Authority + Urgency.definition: The tendency to comply with instructions from a perceived authority figure while simultaneously feeling pressure to act quickly due to imposed time constraints.
Authority + Urgency.phishing_context: Message impersonates an authority figure (e.g., executive leadership or IT security) while imposing time pressure to prompt immediate compliance without verification.
examples: IT Security: Executive leadership has requested that all employees confirm their credentials immediately—complete this verification within the next 20 minutes to avoid account suspension.
From the CFO: Finance needs this payment confirmation submitted right away. Please review and approve within the next 30 minutes.
HR Director Notice: A policy update from leadership requires your acknowledgment today—sign the attached form immediately to remain compliant.

Scarcity + Conformity.definition: The tendency to value limited opportunities more highly while aligning one's behavior with the perceived actions of a larger group.
Scarcity + Conformity.phishing_context: Message claims that a limited opportunity remains while implying that many colleagues have already acted, encouraging the recipient to follow the group before the opportunity disappears.
examples: Only a few remaining employees can enroll in this early benefits update—most of your department has already secured their spot.
Limited beta access to the new company portal is closing soon—many staff members have already activated their accounts.
Only 10 complimentary conference passes remain—your colleagues have already begun claiming theirs.

Overconfidence + Authority.definition: The tendency to overestimate one’s own judgment while simultaneously trusting information from a perceived authority figure.
Overconfidence + Authority.phishing_context: Message flatters the recipient’s expertise while referencing an authoritative source to reduce suspicion and encourage quick approval.
examples: IT Security Team: Given your experience with company systems, you’ve been selected to review this administrative update—simply confirm the details below.
From Executive Leadership: As a trusted senior employee, you’re authorized to quickly validate this internal request without the standard review process.
Security Office: Based on your advanced profile, you’re one of the few users qualified to finalize this compliance check.

Anchoring + Urgency.definition: The tendency to rely heavily on an initial piece of information while simultaneously feeling pressured to act quickly.
Anchoring + Urgency.phishing_context: Message highlights a specific value or reference number to anchor attention while urging immediate action to prevent negative consequences.
examples: Invoice #83921 for $74.23 requires confirmation—please review and approve within the next 15 minutes to avoid escalation.
Security Alert: A $62 transaction was flagged on your account. Verify immediately to prevent automatic lockout.
Your storage usage has reached 1,987 MB of 2,000 MB—upgrade now to avoid service interruption today.

Scarcity + Familiarity.definition: The tendency to trust familiar sources while assigning greater value to opportunities that appear limited.
Scarcity + Familiarity.phishing_context: Message references a familiar service or relationship while claiming the opportunity is available only to a small number of users.
examples: Your usual cloud storage provider is offering a limited account upgrade—only a few existing users can claim it today.
From your regular payroll service: A small number of employees qualify for this new benefits update.
Your campus library account has access to a limited early-release resource—activate it before the remaining licenses are assigned.

Familiarity + Conformity.definition: The tendency to trust familiar sources while aligning behavior with perceived actions taken by a larger group.
Familiarity + Conformity.phishing_context: Message references a familiar service or team environment and implies that many colleagues have already completed the requested action.
examples: Your team’s shared workspace has been updated—most team members have already logged in to review the changes.
The HR portal you normally use has rolled out a new feature—many employees have already completed the update.
Your project group has started using the attached document—please sign in to view what others have added.

Anchoring + Overconfidence.definition: The tendency to rely on a specific initial detail while overestimating one’s ability to quickly judge its legitimacy.
Anchoring + Overconfidence.phishing_context: Message highlights a specific number, transaction, or reference while suggesting the recipient’s expertise should make verification easy.
examples: Invoice #48217 for $58 appears in your records—given your experience, you can quickly confirm whether it’s legitimate.
A $47 reimbursement request was submitted under your account—your familiarity with the process should make this easy to review.
Security Check: Login attempt ID 774293 requires validation—experienced users like you can verify it in seconds.
